message:
Here's a little fact that surprises a lot of cat parents: that chatty "meow" your cat aims at you all day long? They basically save it just for us humans. 🐱💬 Adult cats almost never meow at each other — the running conversation is something they reserve for you.

When cats talk among themselves, they mostly skip the meowing altogether. Instead they rely on scent, body language, tail position, and facial expressions — a whole silent vocabulary they read effortlessly. Meowing is actually something kittens do to get their mom's attention. As cats grow up, they drop it with other cats… but they keep right on doing it with us. Why? Because somewhere along the way they figured out that we humans are a little oblivious to their subtle signals 😹 — and that a good old-fashioned "MEOW" gets the food bowl filled and the door opened much faster.

In other words, your cat has developed a special language just for you. Every meow, chirp, and trill is a phrase in a conversation they invented specifically to talk to their favorite person. That's a pretty big compliment from such an independent little creature. 💛

So tell me about your cat's voice — do you have a chatty Cathy who narrates everything, or a cat of few (but very meaningful) meows? 😺🐾
